items.title: Op deze zender wordt return AZ tegen FC Noah in Conference League uitgezonden
items.summary: AZ speelt donderdagavond de return in de tussenronde van de Conference League. De ploeg van Leeroy Echteld heeft wat goed te maken tegen FC Noah na de 1-0 nederlaag in Armenië. Voetbal International zet uiteen waar dat duel wordt uitgezonden.

items.title: Groenen niet fit, Veurink haalt Nijstad uit voorzorg bij Oranje
items.summary: In dit blog volgen we alle ontwikkelingen in het vrouwenvoetbal. We besteden onder meer aandacht aan de Eurojackpot Vrouwen Eredivisie, de Europese toernooien en het Nederlands elftal.

items.title: Rondje Buitenland Extra: memo voor Koeman en het sprookje van Bodø
items.summary: In een nieuwe aflevering van Rondje Buitenland Extra blikt kenner buitenlands voetbal Süleyman Öztürk uitgebreid terug op de tussenronde van de Champions League. Natuurlijk wordt de spectaculaire stunt van Bodø/Glimt tegen Internazionale besproken, maar ook de prestaties van Marten de Roon bij Atalanta zijn onderwerp van gesprek. Verdient de ervaren middenvelder een nieuwe kans in het Nederlands elftal?

items.title: Man Utd's super sub Sesko banging on door for first start under Carrick
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko has become Manchester United’s super sub after scoring three decisive goals in his last four games – but the £74m striker is banging on the door for his first start under Michael Carrick against Crystal Palace at Old Trafford on Sunday.

items.title: Spaanse media: De Jong valt met blessure uit op training Barça
items.summary: Barcelona dreigt Frenkie de Jong meerdere weken te moeten missen. De 28-jarige middenvelder heeft op de training van donderdag naar verluidt een hamstringblessure opgelopen. De vraag is of dat gevolgen heeft voor de interlandperiode van volgende maand.

items.title: Old Firm onder hoogspanning: 'Wie verliest, ligt eruit'
items.summary: De 450ste editie van The Old Firm heeft alles in huis om een spektakelstuk te worden. De beladen ontmoeting tussen Rangers FC en Celtic staat dit keer in het teken van aanhaken of afhaken in de strijd om het Schotse landskampioenschap. 'Dit is de beste titelstrijd die ik me kan herinneren, zowel als speler als analist. Fascinerend.'

items.title: Real Madrid, PSG or Italian football -- who is worse off?
items.summary: The UEFA Champions League playoffs are over and we're into the last 16. Who has what it takes to win the Champions League? And who struggled in the last round?

items.title: Benn to fight Prograis on Fury-Makhmudov undercard
items.summary: Conor Benn will fight Regis Prograis on the undercard of Tyson Fury's April 11 bout with Arslanbek Makhmudov at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo buys 25% stake in Spanish second division club Almería
items.summary: No financial details of deal with Saudi-owned club Portuguese player already owns share in Al-Nassr Cristiano Ronaldo announced on Thursday that he had acquired a 25% stake in Saudi-owned Spanish second division club Almería. “This strategic investment in UD Almería reflects Ronaldo’s long-term commitment to professional football ownership,” read a statement from his new sports holding company, CR7 Sports Investments, which gave no financial details of the deal. Continue reading...

items.title: Senne Lammens stands tall amid the hurly-burly of United’s ‘war’ games
items.summary: The Belgian goalkeeper has quickly won over the club’s faithful but admits adapting to the Premier League’s physicality has been a challenge Senne Lammens revels in the “war” of being Manchester United’s goalkeeper in the hugely physical Premier League yet remains bemused at being applauded when entering a primary school classroom on a Wednesday afternoon. Clearly the 23-year-old is still adjusting to a rocketing career trajectory. Following his £18m transfer from Royal Antwerp on 1 September, Lammens made his debut in the 2-0 win over Sunderland on 4 October, and established himself as the first-choice with a faultless start that featured five clean sheets in 21 league games. Continue reading...

items.title: The third stage: what to expect from Emma Hayes’s USWNT with World Cup in sight?
items.summary: SheBelieves Cup campaign that starts against Argentina will show coach is now refining rather than experimenting When the whistle blows to start USA v Argentina on Sunday in Nashville, a new period of the Emma Hayes era will begin in earnest. The team preparing to play La Albiceleste in Tennessee for the 11th SheBelieves Cup, followed by Canada and Colombia, is the first in more than a year to feature no uncapped players. For a head coach who spent 2025 setting, challenging or matching all-time USWNT records for capping players, that is a notable shift and it marks the next phase of the team’s World Cup preparation. Continue reading...

items.title: A 'normal guy' doing 'brilliant' things - Lammens on life at Man Utd
items.summary: Manchester United goalkeeper Senne Lammens says he is "just a normal guy", but his reputation is growing after an impressive debut season at Old Trafford.

items.title: England's Rhodes two shots off lead at Women's World Championship
items.summary: England's Mimi Rhodes sits two shots behind early leader Auston Kim after the opening round of the HSBC Women's World Championship in Singapore.

items.title: What ONE football law would you change? Fans have their say, ft. some wild suggestions!
items.summary: Sky Sports Fan Club asked the viewers what ONE law in football they would change - have a listen to their suggestions!!

items.title: A Glimt in the eye: if the plucky Norwegians can do it, why can’t we? | Max Rushden
items.summary: For anyone who supports a club who don’t win anything, the Champions League good guys are delivering the dream Where will you be when Bodø/Glimt win the Champions League? OK, they won’t win the Champions League, but they could win the Champions League. Could they? Four wins in a row. Manchester City, Atlético Madrid away, Inter comprehensively twice. It’s an astonishing run. I am generally cynical about anything foisted upon us by the game’s overlords, but after a brilliant couple of nights of football Uefa must be delighted with the drama and excitement these playoffs produced. Continue reading...

items.title: Ronaldo becomes co-owner of Spanish side Almeria
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes part-owner of Spanish second division side Almeria after acquiring a 25% share in the Saudi Arabian-owned club.

items.title: Ronaldo buys 25% stake in Spanish club Almeria
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has acquired a 25% ownership stake in Spanish second division club UD Almeria.

items.title: Vinícius seals Madrid progress amid Benfica boos
items.summary: Benfica fans loudly booed Real Madrid forward Vinícius Junior at the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium in the second leg of the Champions League playoffs Wednesday.

items.title: Tchouaméni: Prestianni suspension 'victory' for all
items.summary: Real Madrid midfielder Aurélien Tchouaméni believes it was a "victory for all of us" that Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni did not feature in Wednesday's Champions League game at the Bernabéu stadium.

items.title: VAR hands Atalanta winning pen after bloody foul
items.summary: Two red cards, a VAR overturn and a decisive converted penalty with the last kick of the game that came after a bloody foul on Atalanta's Nikola Krstovic.
